text: 1. With the increasing hot-rolling reduction from 45% to 82%, the mean effective grain size including annealing twin boundaries was refined from 21.82 μm to 7.02 μm thanks to the enhanced dynamic recrystallization (DRX), which provided more nucleation sites during hot deformation, and the residual dislocation density after hot rolling increased from $2 . 7 1 \times 1 0 ^ { 1 3 } \mathrm { m } ^ { - 2 } \mathrm { t o } 9 . 8 7 \times 1 0 ^ { 1 3 } \mathrm { m } ^ { - 2 }$ due to the higher unrelieved deformation energy, while the volume fraction of the precipitates barely changed.

2. With the increase in the hot-rolling reduction, the mechanical properties were enhanced from the hot-rolling reduction of 45% to 82% with yield strength and ultimate tensile strength increased from $4 8 0 . 0 \pm 0 . 0$ MPa to 648.0 ± 13.0 MPa and 861.0 ± 11 MPa to 976.0 ± 11.0 MPa, respectively, while the ductility and the Charpy impact energy slightly decreased from $6 0 . 0 \pm 1 . 0 \%$ to $5 0 . 0 \pm 1 . 0 \%$ and 133 $\pm 2 . 5 \mathrm { J / m ^ { 2 } }$ to $1 0 5 \pm 1 . 2 { \mathrm { J } } / { \mathrm { m } } ^ { 2 }$ , respectively. As a result, an excellent combination of strength comparable to that of other aging-hardened low-density austenitic steels and ductility and toughness with a similar level of ductility (\~50%) and toughness $( \sim 1 0 0 \mathrm { ~ J / m ^ { 2 } } )$ to those of other solution-treated austenitic steels.

3. The improvement of yield strength was mainly ascribed to the stronger strengthening effect from grain boundary strengthening and dislocation strengthening. The remaining good ductility and toughness of the present steel after increasing hot-rolling reduction from 45% to 82% were mainly attributed to the maintained continuous slip band refinement process and increased strain-hardening capability at stage II during strain hardening due to the faster refinement of slip bands. In addition, the slight decrease in ductility with increasing hot-rolling reduction was due to the faster development of the deformation substructures revealed by the faster saturation of slip band refinement (slip band spacing reached \~50 nm) and earlier formation of microbands in the samples with finer grain sizes. # Funding This research was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (No.51871062) and the National Key R&D Program of China (No. 2016YFB0300101). # Declaration of Competing Interest The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper. # Data availability Data will be made available on request.
